doc_title: PromptMind Team at EHRSQL-2024: Improving Reliability of SQL Generation using Ensemble LLMs

items.fact: PromptMind Team at EHRSQL-2024 cites Gyubok Lee, Sunjun Kweon, Seongsu Bae, and Edward Choi. 2024.
entities: Gyubok Lee, Sunjun Kweon, Seongsu Bae, and Edward Choi. 2024.
items.evidence_span: Gyubok Lee, Sunjun Kweon, Seongsu Bae, and Edward Choi. 2024. Overview of the ehrsql 2024 shared task on reliable text-to-sql modeling on electronic health records.

items.fact: PromptMind Team at EHRSQL-2024 cites Gyubok Lee, Hyeonji Hwang, Seongsu Bae, Yeonsu Kwon, Woncheol Shin, Seongjun Yang, Minjoon Seo, Jong-Yeup Kim, and Edward Choi. 2022.
entities: Gyubok Lee, Hyeonji Hwang, Seongsu Bae, Yeonsu Kwon, Woncheol Shin, Seongjun Yang, Minjoon Seo, Jong-Yeup Kim, and Edward Choi. 2022.
items.evidence_span: Gyubok Lee, Hyeonji Hwang, Seongsu Bae, Yeonsu Kwon, Woncheol Shin, Seongjun Yang, Minjoon Seo, Jong-Yeup Kim, and Edward Choi. 2022. Ehrsql: A practical text-to-sql benchmark for electronic health records.

items.fact: PromptMind Team at EHRSQL-2024 cites Wang et al. (2020)
items.evidence_span: Prior to the advent of LLMs, the primary focus of research in natural language processing involved refining specialized models using innovative strategies (Wang et al., 2020).

items.fact: PromptMind Team at EHRSQL-2024 cites Cai et al. (2022)
items.evidence_span: Additionally, substantial efforts were devoted to developing sophisticated pre-training methodologies, such as those proposed by STAR (Cai et al., 2022),

items.fact: PromptMind Team at EHRSQL-2024 cites Scholak et al. (2021)
items.evidence_span: and exploring decoding strategies, as exemplified by PICARD (Scholak et al., 2021).

items.fact: PromptMind Team at EHRSQL-2024 cites Dong et al. (2023)
items.evidence_span: Several prompt techniques have been developed based on this zero-shot approach for Textto-SQL tasks, leading to remarkable achievements on datasets such as SPIDER (Dong et al., 2023),

items.fact: PromptMind Team at EHRSQL-2024 cites Liu et al. (2023)
PromptMind Team at EHRSQL-2024 cites Pourreza and Rafiei (2023)
items.evidence_span: DIN-SQL (Pourreza and Rafiei, 2023) adopts an in-context learning approach to break down complex SQL generation into manageable subtasks,

items.fact: PromptMind Team at EHRSQL-2024 cites Guo et al. (2024)
items.evidence_span: Another technique, retrieval-augmented generation, provides relevant and helpful examples as a few-shot to guide SQL generation (Guo et al., 2024).

items.fact: PromptMind Team at EHRSQL-2024 cites Lewis et al. (2020)
items.evidence_span: Retrieval Augmented Finetuning (RAFT) introduces a novel fine-tuning technique that improves the in-domain performance of RAG while integrating domain-specific knowledge (Lewis et al., 2020).

items.fact: EHRSQL is the first dataset that closely captures the needs of hospital staff and serves appropriately for building and testing Text-to-SQL systems in the healthcare domain (Lee et al., 2022).
items.evidence_span: EHRSQL is the first dataset that closely captures the needs of hospital staff and serves appropriately for building and testing Text-to-SQL systems in the healthcare domain (Lee et al., 2022).

items.fact: BIRD-Bench was introduced to bridge the gap between research and real-world applications by providing large and imperfect databases (Li et al., 2024).
items.evidence_span: BIRD-Bench was introduced to bridge the gap between research and real-world applications by providing large and imperfect databases (Li et al., 2024).

items.fact: Subsequently, the SPIDER (Yu et al., 2018) and MULTI-SPIDER (Dou et al., 2023) datasets were developed.
items.evidence_span: Subsequently, the SPIDER (Yu et al., 2018) and MULTI-SPIDER (Dou et al., 2023) datasets were developed.

items.fact: Introduced in 2017, WikiSQL (Zhong et al., 2017) remains one of the largest datasets for Text-to-SQL and primarily caters to relatively simple queries.
items.evidence_span: Introduced in 2017, WikiSQL (Zhong et al., 2017) remains one of the largest datasets for Text-to-SQL and primarily caters to relatively simple queries.
